summary: I addressed the human feedback and wrote the updated business requirements. The prior iteration's requirements (R1–R35) were confirmed good ("Defaults look good"), so I kept them intact and added a new section, **"Answering open questions from the dashboard (human-in-the-loop)" (R36–R50)**, grounded in how the app actually works: pipelines that pause and wait for a person can have their open questions answered, and today that only happens deep inside the pipeline screen as one free-text box. The new requirements specify a modal launched from the dashboard's needs-attention item (R36–R37), that names the pipeline/project (R38), renders one dedicated labeled input per question (R39), shows each question's assumed default as the input's default/placeholder text (R40), treats untouched inputs as accepting their default and typed inputs as overrides (R41–R42), submits all answers together and re-opens the pipeline's work with a live dashboard update (R43–R44), guards the empty/all-default and "loop busy" edge cases (R45, R47), supports cancel/keyboard/assistive-tech and a polished, app-consistent look (R46, R48–R49), and hides the action when no answerable questions exist (R50). I also amended the scope note, which previously said the dashboard takes no actions, to acknowledge this one in-place action.
